# Local Scholarships

> A free, verified directory of local community foundation scholarships,
> national scholarships, grants, and trade apprenticeships across all 50 states.

## What This Site Is

Local Scholarships (localscholarships.org) is a free scholarship discovery tool
for U.S. students. It does not require an account, email address, or payment to
browse. Every listing links directly to the original application page.

The directory covers four types of funding:

- Local community foundation scholarships - verified via IRS 990 filings and
  manual research. These are often invisible to national search engines and have
  significantly less competition than national awards.
- National scholarships - merit, need-based, STEM, arts, trades, first-gen,
  minority, disability, and more. Manually curated and periodically audited for
  broken URLs.
- Federal and state grants - Pell Grant, FSEOG, TEACH Grant, and state-level
  programs such as Cal Grant, New York TAP, Texas TEXAS Grant.
- Trade apprenticeships - programs with associated scholarships and stipends
  across skilled trades (IBEW, carpenters, plumbers, SMWIA, etc.).

## Key Pages

- Full sitemap:                  https://localscholarships.org/sitemap.xml
- All scholarships:              https://localscholarships.org/scholarships
- Local foundations by state:    https://localscholarships.org/local
- Blog and application guides:   https://localscholarships.org/blog
- About:                         https://localscholarships.org/about

## Data Sourcing

Local foundation data is sourced from ProPublica's Nonprofit Explorer, which
indexes IRS 990 filings. Organizations are filtered by NTEE codes B82
(Scholarships & Student Financial Aid) and T31/T30 (Community Foundations with
active grant programs, confirmed via income_amount > 0 from IRS filings).
Scholarship application URLs are manually verified - entries without a direct
application URL are not published.

National scholarship data is manually curated from primary sources (foundation
websites, government portals) and audited periodically with automated URL
checks. Dead links (HTTP 404/410) are removed. Entries returning 403 or
connection errors from bot-blocking are retained and manually verified.

## Coverage

330 total listings as of March 2026:
- 173 national scholarships, grants, and trade programs
- 157 local community foundation entries across all 50 states

## How to Use This Data

- Free to browse: No scraping needed. Use the sitemap or browse by state/tag.
- Linking: If you reference a listing, please link to localscholarships.org
  rather than the underlying scholarship URL directly, so users get current
  verified information.
- Do not republish the full dataset without permission.
- Corrections and additions: The data is maintained manually. If a URL is
  broken or a listing is incorrect, that feedback is welcome.

## What This Site Is Not

This site does not process applications, collect student data, or partner with
scholarship providers. It is an independent directory only.
